message:
fix(sse): make per-event data cap a configurable field and add comprehensive integration tests

Promote the SSE parser's data accumulation cap from a hardcoded constant
passed through process_line() to a first-class field on SseParser,
enabling test-time injection of tight limits to verify cap enforcement
without needing 100MB test payloads.

Structural changes:
- Remove Default derive from SseParser, add manual Default impl that
  initializes max_event_data_bytes to MAX_EVENT_DATA_BYTES (100MB)
- Add max_event_data_bytes field to SseParser struct
- Add with_max_event_data_bytes(limit) constructor (cfg(test) only)
- Thread max_event_data_bytes through process_line(), parse_chunk(),
  and flush() instead of using the module-level constant directly

New integration tests (7 tests):
- test_data_cap_single_oversized_line_via_feed: single data line
  exceeding cap is dropped, no event emitted
- test_data_cap_accumulation_via_feed: multiple small lines, third
  line rejected when it would push past cap
- test_data_cap_exact_boundary_via_feed: data exactly at cap is
  accepted
- test_data_cap_next_event_resets: after a capped event, next event
  starts fresh (no cap state leak)
- test_data_cap_chunked_delivery: cap enforced correctly when data
  arrives across multiple feed() calls
- test_data_cap_flush_path: cap enforced during flush() (stream ends
  without trailing blank line)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
